evals.prompt: The fastmcp-creator skill hasn't been updated since March and fastmcp is now on v3.3. Can you sync it against the current upstream docs?
evals.expected_output: Pipeline runs all 6 stages: token profile, 3 parallel read agents, synthesis with change plan, clean-tree gate, source-validator agent pre-fetch, write agent applies changes. Modified files pass skilllint.

evals.prompt: Run skill-sync on plugins/plugin-creator/skills/skill-creator to check if any SOURCE: URLs have gone stale
evals.expected_output: Stage 2 drift scanner fetches all SOURCE: citations, classifies each as VERIFIED/STALE/NEW/UNVERIFIABLE, produces drift report. If all VERIFIED, exits with no-changes plan. If STALE, produces change plan and runs through write stage.

evals.prompt: I want to sync the entire plugin-creator plugin against upstream — all its skills need their docs refreshed
evals.expected_output: Argument resolved as plugin directory. Pipeline runs once per skill found under skills/. Each skill gets its own change plan. Modified files committed per skill or as a batch.

Pipeline runs for the uv skill. Stage 1 profiles token budget. Stage 2 dispatches 3 agents. If SOURCE: URLs cite uv docs, drift scanner fetches and classifies them. Change plan written to .tmp/scratch/plans/. Stages 4.5 and 5 run if changes found.

evals.prompt: Check if my aws-deploy skill's documentation is still accurate — the AWS CLI changed their syntax last month
evals.expected_output: Pipeline treats this as a sync request. Argument parsing resolves the skill path. Drift scanner fetches SOURCE: URLs and compares against current upstream. Report produced with any STALE or NEW findings. No write stage unless changes confirmed.
